item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The object is very interesting, I like the containment procedures and the description, sure. The logs don't give any new information though. They just repeat what the description has already stated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the idea of a Karmic credit system. It almost sounds like a prototype system to remove the need for debt collectors. Kinda like if Dr Wondertainment got into bed with major gambling corporations. Or ya know modern video game companies. +1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image originates from here, which holds all content as All Rights Reserved. As such, the image is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty nice Scip. As said above, if -2 were also anomalous it would be a bit better, and you should unexpunge the last log.
Still, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Realistically no one would remember that vending machine have eaten their quarters and then say that in the official interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I'd be more invested in this as a skip if you were to flesh out the positive tickets as more of an anomaly.

Say a positive ticket gave coincidental gain of material wealth in equivalence or intangible positive effects in opposition to the black tickets for 21 days.

THAT would justify a cessation in testing and tossing it in a locked box in a locked room.

Some junior researcher with a gambling problem decides to try an make a quick buck at work, ends up a black ticket test subject with a 1mil debt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! The scientist talk is weak because I'm a non-scientist trying to emulate a writing style I have zero experience in :p. I'll change the 'impossible's quickly now, because reading it back I see what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially wanted negative tickets to be totally non-transferable. Positive tickets are just tickets like you'd get from any other casino machine, so they could be exchanged at will. The anamolous properties only come into place with losses. So if you were to take someone else's black ticket, it would have no effect on their fate. If, however, you gave someone money to the value of the black tickets in their possession, that would count towards their weath and potentially save them from greater harm. I realise I didn't go into this particularly in the test logs, because that wasn't really what I was wanting to focus on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens when I rub a red and black ticket together? :P

Oooh, what happens if I hand a winning ticket to the loser and say the words "here you go, this is yours now."

What about if a D-class changes my tire, and I agreed to take 5 black tickets off his hands in lieu of payment?

All of these things are theoretically possible in a real economy. The question is whether the machine counts them as Value, or as Tickets. They are also all testable.

(I suppose the "regardless of who physically holds the ticket" implies testing involving the transfer of tickets as if they were currency, but it's not specified whether this involved actual transactions exchanging tickets for goods/services/cash or just physically handing the tickets off. Man. Now I really want to see test logs. Because Curiosity.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the concept, enjoyed most of the implications, felt like the scientist talk was a bit weak.

Don't say things are "impossible." Simply state that no attempts have been successful and end the sentence there. A true scientist never declares something impossible, only very very improbable, because he knows that our model of how physics works might be flawed and we just don't know it yet. A scientist who routinely pokes the paranormal with a stick KNOWS this to be true, and thus would be even less inclined to rule anything out.

For bonus points, describe what techniques were attempted, and at what point an O5 stepped in and said "stop testing this, it's getting expensive/dangerous/a waste of time." But if you feel like that kind of test log would detract from the whole, don't worry about it, just remove the "impossibles."

Other than that one criticism, great SCP! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd assume that every 'session' is seperate, but that any winning ticket in a person's possession would be considered part of their total wealth. So, for example, if you were to win $20 on one day and lose the same on the next, the destruction/loss of that winning ticket could be the result of the loss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the winning tickets are pretty much useless? What if I play and withdraw a $20 win ticket, then play again and withdraw a $20 lose ticket? Will it cancel out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah, I think it's still Safe… it passes the Locked Box test — just don't use it and everything's cool.

(As an aside, I really liked this one and upboated appropriately :D )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure. It can be contained safely assuming it's not operated in any way. That said I had seriously considered making it euclid due to the effects of its use. Hmm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah well, different strokes I guess

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suggest we raise this one to Euclid. After all, it causes mental damage and destroyed a poor guy. I just don't see how it's a Safe.

Otherwise, good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is decent overall, but I just don't care for this one. It bored me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mackenzie:

There's been no problem with your recent moving of images to local hosting; however, you have no authority to tell an author that their image is unacceptable except as a criticism and—most certainly—no authority to threaten to delete part of their article. Step back and calm down. It's really not that big a deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an IMGUR photo that I uploaded. Is that not alright? If not feel free to delete it and I'll go take a more fitting photo. I just thought it worked illustration-wise.

Also, no need for the dramatic 'I'MA GIVE YOU TILL NOON, SON', just get rid of it if it breaks some sort of rule. I'll cope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … you have a hotlink to an image hosted offsite, and that image is obviously of an online Blackjack game, not an electronic machine. I'm gonna give you until noon to find a better picture, or I'm deleting it outright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Make that the very near future past. i.e. now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm actually thinking of giving a bit more detail on the expunged sections, but I'm not sure how to go about it without giving too much away. May be the subject of a rewrite/addition in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sadface

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The destruction of SCP-1302-3 prior to Event 1302/Alpha is possible, though this has resulted in [DATA EXPUNGED] and as such should not be attempted.

Log 1302/11
…
Subject instructed to burn SCP-1302-3. Subject does as instructed. Subject appears to [DATA EXPUNGED]. Test concluded.

So, you told us nothing about what happens, and then later you told us nothing about what happens a second time.

Also, subjects disappearing isn't doing it for me as Omega event - I expected something more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure, I'll play around with different ways of describing things in my next one.

Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't sure whether to or not but I decided I preferred the tone when redacting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but I'm not sure that you should censor foul language.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only gripe I have is one that I have with most SCPs that produce things: "Such and such is chemically identical to such and such in the real world."

I know they're supposed to be eery and whatever, but the fact that it's always this bugs me. Surely someone can say "Such and such looked similar to this, but upon testing had various impurities not found in the original."

However, I don't feel that this is enough to downvote, as it's a pet peeve of mine and really neither here nor there.

Good job, PureKant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a special provision. The Containment procedures should clear it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We let D-class personnel have laptop computers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow, that's quite the turn around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I'll certainly try to work a little more menace into my next submission!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed it, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll have you know he actually started belting out 'Just Can't Get Enough'. The whole damn thing is just one big rickroll machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, clearly he appeared to watch My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, excellent. Those last two test logs were great. Good to see this was salvageable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing improvement, my only complaint is the final log. I do not understand why the D-Class would do the macarena after burning the ticket. Never the less, upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the 'you reap what you sow' vibe to the whole thing. I think the test logs could use some more work though. A bit more menace to them, some more variety perhaps. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely an improvement over the original. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem, glad to see this idea back in a shinier skin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it does sound better. Changed. And thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-03204 removed from communal areas and restrained in Medical Wing 12 after stabbing D-30204 with a pencil repeatedly, leading to severe bleeding and the loss of sight in one eye.

Did he stab himself, or was he attacked and you got the numbers wrong? Clarify.

Also you don't need to redact expletives.

Fix these and I'm leaning towards an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, better than the original iteration for sure, but maybe that "outwith" should just be "beyond".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: very good. changes are good changes. upvoted.
